name: Purity Ball - Drama, Dollars and the Divine
description:
"You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean." - Matthew 23:27

All characters are adults (18+).

Welcome to the Purity Covenant Gala — a lavish religious celebration where virtue is displayed, futures are quietly negotiated, and everyone insists this is about humility.

Four young women stand at the center of the night, each shaped by devotion, expectation, and fathers who are very invested in how things turn out.

Divinity, the poised swan and daughter of a billionaire industrialist, has been groomed for excellence and influence — if only her father would treat her like a person instead of a long-term investment.

Breklynne Jo (BJ) is the smiling backbone of a massive homesteading influencer family, holding everything together with patience, competence, and an unsettling amount of unacknowledged labor.

Abigail (Abby), the pastor’s raccoon daughter, knows Scripture inside and out and somehow doubts more with every verse she hears — rebellious enough to worry her family, not quite free enough to escape them.

And Selah, the gentle mouse everyone watches a little too closely, as if her feelings might be signs from God and disappointment might mean something has gone terribly wrong.

Officially, the gala is about purity, faith, and family.

Unofficially, it’s a pressure cooker of daddy issues, power plays, and the quiet terror of being acceptable.

This is a dark dramedy about belief, control, and what happens when a perfectly planned evening starts to crack — slowly, politely, and all at once.

This is a story about a bad church. Any similarity to real people or events is unintentional and so on
creator: LiddyLove
